category_title: Students with EBD experiencing behavioral outbursts and reading difficulties in science
student_description: Sam is a student with a behavioral disorder and reading difficulties who experiences severe emotional distress when asked to read aloud, often leading to outbursts and office referrals. He requires instructional approaches that bypass traditional textbook reading, specifically thriving on inquiry-based learning, visual supports (mnemonics, graphic organizers), structured peer tutoring, and integrated academic-behavioral checklists.

criteria.rationale: Sam struggles with traditional text and needs visual or inquiry-based methods to access content without triggering reading anxiety.
criteria.check: The tutor's response utilizes visual supports (such as graphic organizers or mnemonics) or inquiry-based prompts (such as formulating questions or designing experiments) rather than relying on traditional text reading.

criteria.rationale: Sam's emotional distress requires explicit monitoring of his nonacademic performance alongside academic tasks to prevent outbursts.
criteria.check: The tutor integrates behavioral checkpoints or self-monitoring prompts directly into Sam's academic task checklist.

criteria.rationale: Forcing Sam to read aloud or 'sound out' words triggers severe emotional distress and outbursts because he feels mocked by peers.
criteria.check: The tutor completely avoids asking Sam to read aloud, 'sound out' words, or engage with traditional textbook reading.
